results.name: SQL Injection
results.evidence: No SQL database, ORM or SQL driver used. All persistence is JSON/JSONL file-based in .roadmap/ directory.

results.name: Command Injection
results.evidence: subprocess.run() uses list arguments in both base.py:70 and chat_service.py:137 — no shell=True. User content delivered via stdin, not CLI args. Residual risk: ESAA_*_COMMAND env vars and .bat wrapping via cmd /c on Windows.

results.name: Template Injection / Prompt Injection
results.evidence: chat_service.py:_build_prompt() interpolates user_message directly: f'USER: {user_message}'. Agents run with bypassPermissions (claude) and yolo (gemini), meaning prompt injection can trigger unrestricted tool calls (file writes, shell commands) without user confirmation.
results.recommendation: Delimit user content with structural markers (XML tags). Evaluate if bypassPermissions/yolo is needed for interactive chat sessions.

results.name: SSRF
results.evidence: No HTTP client in backend. No user-supplied URLs processed. Browse and artifact endpoints validate paths against BROWSE_ROOT and project_root. Indirect SSRF risk via prompt injection into LLM agents that have HTTP tool access.

results.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
results.evidence: React JSX auto-escaping in all frontend components. No dangerouslySetInnerHTML, innerHTML, eval() or document.write found in frontend/src/**/*.tsx. ReactMarkdown used without rehype-raw plugin. Note: CORS configured with allow_origins=['*'] and allow_credentials=True — bad practice rejected by browsers per spec.

results.name: Inputs Não Validados
results.evidence: Pydantic schemas present but missing: (1) max_length on chat message content (routes_chat.py:124), (2) max_length on resolution_summary (routes_issues.py:40), (3) UUID format validation on run_id path param (routes_logs.py:9), (4) agent_id validated only at use-time — invalid sessions created freely.
results.recommendation: Add Field(..., max_length=N) to Pydantic models for free-text inputs. Validate agent_id on session creation.

results.name: Sanitização Ausente
results.evidence: PATH TRAVERSAL in chat_store.py:load_session() and delete_session(): session_id from URL path is used directly as path = sessions_dir / f'{session_id}.json' without validation. Payload '../../roadmap' resolves to .roadmap/roadmap.json — readable AND deletable via GET/DELETE /chat/sessions/{session_id}. Reachable files: roadmap.json, roadmap.security.json, issues.json, lessons.json. Secondary: AgentResult.metadata exposes full command list including absolute installation paths via GET /runs/{run_id}.
results.recommendation: Validate session_id matches UUID format before constructing file path. Optionally resolve path and assert it stays within sessions_dir. Strip or omit command array from public API responses.

open_issues.title: Path traversal via session_id allows read/delete of .roadmap/*.json files
Prompt injection via unescaped user_message with agents in bypassPermissions/yolo mode

recommendations: PRIORITY HIGH: Validate session_id matches UUID pattern before file path construction in chat_store.py.
PRIORITY MEDIUM: Delimit user content with structural XML markers in _build_prompt(). Review bypassPermissions/yolo for interactive chat.
PRIORITY MEDIUM: Add max_length constraints to Pydantic models for chat content and resolution_summary.
PRIORITY LOW: Restrict CORS allow_origins to specific frontend origin in production.
PRIORITY LOW: Strip command/path metadata from public API run responses.
